opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My gums have been bleeding continuously since dinner, my mouth is full of blood, and I am too weak to even stand up from this chair. (Reported jointly by the patient and their spouse.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The gums started bleeding on their own this evening and won't stop, and I've become so drained I can't get out of the chair.

symptom_narrative.onset: the gum bleeding started this evening after dinner with no injury; the exhaustion has been building for about 4 months and got dramatically worse over the last 2 days
symptom_narrative.duration: gum bleeding for a few hours tonight; profound tiredness for months, sharply worse in the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: slow continuous oozing of blood from the gums, and a crushing whole-body weakness and light-headedness
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst I've ever felt — I can't stand up and my mouth keeps filling with blood
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: bleeding from the gums; the weakness and dizziness are all over
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the bleeding has been constant since dinner; the tiredness is constant and much worse when I try to stand
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing has helped — the iron and vitamin tablets from my GP made no difference at all over months
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up makes me dizzy and feel like I'll pass out
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: my spouse noticed new pinpoint red spots on my ankles today, and I've got some big bruises on my arms I don't remember causing; I also had a nasty unexplained chest infection last month
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no bleeding like this before, but I've been getting more and more worn out for months despite the iron and B12
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm stuck in the chair, too weak and dizzy to walk to the bathroom safely

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: months of worsening tiredness that my GP was treating as anaemia with iron and vitamin B12
past_medical_history.how_managed: prescribed oral iron and vitamin B12 by my regular GP, taken as directed with no improvement

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a severe unexplained chest infection last month that I have since recovered from

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with spouse who is present and helping report; no blood-thinning medicines or anti-inflammatory painkillers taken

objective_data_offered.value: on camera there is slow steady oozing of blood from the gum margins and the patient is holding a blood-stained tissue to their mouth; they look very pale and washed out
new pinpoint red spots on both ankles and lower legs, and several large bruises on the forearms that weren't knocked
too weak and dizzy to stand from the chair without feeling faint
37.1 C on a home thermometer this evening, no fever
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an uncalibrated home thermometer
